text: The task says footer "on the task page," but the plan puts it in the shared `_render_base()` so it appears on home, task, and stage config pages too. Which scope do you want?
options: All pages via `_render_base` (home + task + stage config)

text: What exactly should the footer display as the "server name"? The plan hardcodes the literal string "crack-pi-server" (matching pyproject `name`), but it could be a runtime hostname or a config value instead.
options: A configurable value (env var / config file)

text: The plan's verification uses `grep -c 'crack-pi-server' == 1`, but the string may appear in other places (homepage task cards, page title, etc.), making the count fragile. Also, the actual `_render_base` has NO `class="container"` on `<main>` (it's Pico *classless*), and there's a `<script src="/static/app.js">` after `</main>`. Confirm: should I trust the ACTUAL code (line 77, no container class, script after main) over the plan's sketch?
options: Yes — re-read actual code and fix the plan's sketch before implementing
No — proceed with the plan's sketched structure as-is

text: Styling approach: the plan offers both inline styles AND an optional `.page-footer` CSS class as alternatives. Which do you prefer?
options: Inline styles only (no CSS file change)
CSS class in app.css (cleaner, matches existing .prompt-row/.title-row pattern)
Inline now, extract to CSS class in same change

text: The footer placement: inside `<main>` (after content, before `</main>`) as the plan states, or after `</main>`/before `</body>`? Task page content can be tall (htmx-swapped explore/plan sections) — a footer inside `<main>` will scroll with content rather than stick to viewport bottom.
options: Inside `<main>` after the body content (scrolls with page)
After `</main>`, before `</body>` (still at page bottom but outside main)
